title: Curl not found in Execute Command node
symptom: When executing a command in the Execute Command node, n8n returns an error stating '/bin/sh: curl: not found'. This indicates that the curl utility is not available in the Docker container.
applies_when: Running n8n in a Docker container
not_this_if: Curl is installed in the Docker image
Using a different command that does not require curl
root_cause: The n8n Docker images do not include package managers like apt-get or apk, which means that utilities like curl are not available by default. This is common in minimal Docker images to reduce size and complexity.
fix_steps: Modify your Dockerfile to include the necessary commands to install curl.
Use the provided solution to copy apk from an Alpine image and install curl using apk.
agent_instruction: Update your Dockerfile to install curl by copying apk from an Alpine image and using it to install curl.
